reasoning: A vaccine for Coxsackie B virus (such as PRV-101) has only recently completed Phase 1 trials (in early 2024). Moving from Phase 1 to full approval and clinical use virtually always takes multiple years, meaning there is practically zero chance a vaccine will be fully developed, approved, and added to the 'List of infectious diseases' Wikipedia page by February 2026. The only real pathways to a 'Yes' resolution involve either extreme abbreviation of the clinical trial process (which is highly improbable) or erroneous edits (vandalism/misinformation) to the Wikipedia page on the exact resolution dates that go un-reverted. Consequently, the probability is set at the minimum allowable threshold for the near term, with only slight upward adjustments for later horizons to account for marginally increased uncertainty over time.
